title: The One with the Two Parties

jokes.text: Question. Why do we always have parties where you poach things?
jokes.setup: Monica mentions poaching salmon for Rachel's birthday party
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sardonic observation about Monica's fancy cooking habits
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm with good character integration - his resistance to pretentious food is established. Strong structure and economy.

jokes.text: Question 2. Why do we always have parties with committees?
jokes.setup: Follows directly from his first complaint about poaching
jokes.punchline: Escalates his criticism to the entire party structure
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from the previous joke, maintaining momentum and character consistency.

jokes.text: I agree. Fancy parties are only fun if you're fancy on the inside. And I'm not sure we are.
jokes.setup: Chandler's complaints about fancy parties
jokes.punchline: Joey's unexpectedly philosophical take with self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Joey being unexpectedly insightful about class and authenticity is perfectly in character yet surprising.

jokes.text: Wow, it's like porno for clowns!
jokes.setup: Joey discovering something about fake body parts
jokes.punchline: Chandler's absurd comparison combining two unexpected elements
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor with perfect economy. The combination of 'porno' and 'clowns' is unexpectedly hilarious and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Or maybe she doesn't and Joey slept with her and never called her back.
jokes.setup: Monica claims Shannon Cooper steals stuff
jokes.punchline: Chandler cuts to the real reason they can't invite her
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reveals character history and Chandler's insight into Joey's patterns.

jokes.text: Hey, I liked her. Maybe too much. I don't know. I guess I just got scared. I'm sorry. I didn't know. I didn't think anyone would buy that. Okay!
jokes.setup: Joey appears to give a sincere, vulnerable explanation
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was completely making it up
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with a fake-out emotional moment that's perfectly in character for Joey's casual approach to relationships.

jokes.text: My cousin went to hell on a football scholarship.
jokes.setup: Rachel says graduation was 'hell'
jokes.punchline: Phoebe takes it literally and adds absurd detail
jokes.explanation: Peak Phoebe - perfect literal interpretation with an absurd twist. Economy is flawless and it's completely character-specific.

jokes.text: It's a testament to what a girl from Long Island will do for a Celica.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining her sister's motivation for graduating
jokes.punchline: Reduces academic achievement to materialistic motivation
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about class and materialism with specific detail (Celica) that makes it feel authentic.

jokes.text: Bishop Tutu actually had to stop and shush them.
jokes.setup: Parents fighting during graduation
jokes.punchline: Escalates to involving a famous religious figure
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with a specific cultural reference that adds absurdity to the family embarrassment.

jokes.text: Happy birthday, Rachel. Here's some goo!
jokes.setup: Monica explains flan as 'traditional Mexican custard dessert'
jokes.punchline: Chandler reduces it to unappetizing 'goo'
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deflating pretension with blunt honesty. Good economy and character-specific humor.

jokes.text: No, no, not a party. Just a surprise gathering of some people Rachel knows.
jokes.setup: Dr. Greene notices they're having a party
jokes.punchline: Monica's desperate attempt to redefine what's obviously a party
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Monica's tendency to over-explain and control situations.

jokes.text: This isn't your first surprise party, is it, sir?
jokes.setup: Dr. Greene asks basic questions about how surprise parties work
jokes.punchline: Chandler's politely sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery with Chandler's characteristic polite sarcasm toward authority figures.

jokes.text: The Chinese menu guy. Forgot the menus. So basically, just a Chinese guy.
jokes.setup: Someone at the door turns out not to be the Chinese food delivery
jokes.punchline: Chandler's logical but absurd conclusion
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic with perfect economy. The racial element is handled through Chandler's matter-of-fact delivery rather than malice.

jokes.text: That sounds like a two-person job.
jokes.setup: Monica offers to put Dr. Greene's jacket on Rachel's bed
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry observation about the simple task
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo with good delivery but fairly predictable setup.

jokes.text: Yes, because you look so young. And because you're both, you know, white women.
jokes.setup: Monica explains why she confused Rachel's mom with Rachel
jokes.punchline: Escalates from compliment to awkward racial observation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Monica's tendency to over-explain and make things awkward.

jokes.text: What would Jack and Chrissy do?
jokes.setup: Monica panicking about managing two separate parties
jokes.punchline: References Three's Company, another sitcom about apartment mix-ups
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-reference that acknowledges the show's debt to classic sitcom farce situations.

jokes.text: Why, again?
jokes.setup: Monica tells Joey and Chandler to take Dr. Greene to their apartment
jokes.punchline: Joey's confused response showing he hasn't been following the plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his confusion is completely in character and economically delivered.

jokes.text: Because that's where the party is, you goon!
jokes.setup: Joey's confusion about the plan
jokes.punchline: Monica's exasperated response with mild insult
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's stress but the insult 'goon' feels somewhat dated and mild.

jokes.text: This is clearly in the wrong apartment.
jokes.setup: Being told this apartment is just the 'staging area'
jokes.punchline: Dr. Greene's matter-of-fact observation of the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good dry delivery that punctures the obvious lie, but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: At least send some women to my party.
jokes.setup: Being sent to the 'dad party' with Dr. Greene
jokes.punchline: Joey's priority is women, not the party itself
jokes.explanation: Very much in character for Joey - his priorities are clear and consistent.

jokes.text: Now, you can exchange them if you want, okay? Now I love you even more.
jokes.setup: Ross gives Rachel earrings and she says she loves him
jokes.punchline: Ross offers exchange option, Rachel's love increases
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment with mild humor about gift-giving practicality vs. romance.

jokes.text: Really? No, I knew.
jokes.setup: Claims to be surprised by the party
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits she wasn't surprised
jokes.explanation: Quick misdirection that's honest but deflates the surprise party moment slightly.

jokes.text: Both of them are here? Both of them? Both of them are here?
jokes.setup: Rachel realizes both divorced parents are at her parties
jokes.punchline: Her panicked repetition shows increasing stress
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's stress effectively but the repetition is more dramatic than comedic.

jokes.text: Well, we could count again?
jokes.setup: Rachel's obvious distress about both parents being there
jokes.punchline: Chandler's literal, unhelpful suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - completely unhelpful but delivered with characteristic deadpan logic.

jokes.text: Well, actually... just one birthday flan. It's a traditional Mexican custard dessert. Talk to Monica. She's on the food committee.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentions getting two birthday cakes
jokes.punchline: Ross deflates expectations while passing blame to Monica
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces the flan running gag and Monica's control issues.

jokes.text: Some girl just walked up to me and said, 'I want you, Dennis'... and stuck her tongue down my throat. I love this party!
jokes.setup: Chandler at the fun party gets mistaken for someone else
jokes.punchline: He doesn't care about the mistake, just enjoys the attention
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's romantic desperation and ability to go with the flow.

jokes.text: You didn't like that gray lamp, did you?
jokes.setup: Setting up volleyball court in someone's room
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of breaking/moving furniture
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy implication showing the party's gotten out of hand, very Joey-like casual destruction.

jokes.text: Stay clear of 'I'm the guy that's doing your daughter'... and you should be okay.
jokes.setup: Ross asks for conversation openers with Rachel's dad
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deliberately inappropriate suggestion
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler advice - technically helpful while being completely inappropriate. Good character work.

jokes.text: When you're not using the markers, put the caps back on them... because they will dry out.
jokes.setup: Monica organizing party games
jokes.punchline: Her obsessive attention to marker maintenance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica control freak moment - obsessing over minor details during a party.

jokes.text: It's not a game, Ross. A woman died on my table today. That's the good thing about my job. All the dinosaurs on my table are already dead.
jokes.setup: Ross awkwardly calls surgery a 'game'
jokes.punchline: Dr. Greene's dark reality vs Ross's lighter paleontology work
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor contrasting the gravity of medicine with paleontology. Well-structured with good payoff.

jokes.text: You want to push the caps... until you hear them click.
jokes.setup: Earlier marker cap obsession
jokes.punchline: Monica becomes even more specific about cap placement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Monica's obsessive behavior, showing her inability to let things go.

jokes.text: She'll yell at me again.
jokes.setup: Gunther wants to leave Monica's party
jokes.punchline: Reveals Monica has already yelled at him
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's controlling nature has intimidated even Gunther.

jokes.text: In a minute, I'll create a diversion. When I do, walk quickly to the door and don't look back.
jokes.setup: Gunther wants to escape Monica's party
jokes.punchline: Chandler treats it like a prison break or spy mission
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation treating a party departure like an escape mission. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I'll be back in 10 seconds with your scotch on the rocks in a glass. Neat! Cool. Neat, as in no rocks. I know.
jokes.setup: Taking Dr. Greene's drink order
jokes.punchline: Ross misunderstands 'neat' then overexplains his understanding
jokes.explanation: Good Ross moment showing his nervous tendency to overexplain and misunderstand simple things.

jokes.text: Stay clear of the salmon mousse.
jokes.setup: Ross coming from the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Implies he's been sick from Monica's fancy food
jokes.explanation: Good economy and setup - implies digestive issues from fancy party food without being explicit.

jokes.text: Mine too. Isn't that neat? Scotch neat.
jokes.setup: Discussion of scotch preferences
jokes.punchline: Ross makes a pun on 'neat' from earlier confusion
jokes.explanation: Mild callback to earlier wordplay, shows Ross trying to recover from his earlier mistake.

jokes.text: What a great moment to say that for the first time.
jokes.setup: Someone says something awkward for the first time
jokes.punchline: Chandler's observation about timing
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler observation about social awkwardness and poor timing.

jokes.text: Monica, your party sucks.
jokes.setup: Reading embarrassing memory cards
jokes.punchline: Someone's brutal honesty about the party
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest and economical - captures guest frustration with Monica's controlling party style.

jokes.text: Oh, no! Did someone forget to use a coaster?
jokes.setup: Monica's obsessive party hosting
jokes.punchline: Her overreaction to a water ring
jokes.explanation: Peak Monica obsessiveness - caring more about coasters than guests having fun.

jokes.text: Great, I'm seeing water rings again.
jokes.setup: Monica's coaster obsession
jokes.punchline: Implies she's had therapy for furniture anxiety
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation suggesting Monica's cleaning obsession is pathological. Great character specificity.

jokes.text: I have a condition apparently... that I require... two different sets of focals.
jokes.setup: Caught wearing Dr. Greene's bifocals
jokes.punchline: Ross's awkward medical-sounding explanation
jokes.explanation: Good Ross panic moment - his tendency to sound pseudo-medical when nervous.

jokes.text: Neil Sedaka wears them.
jokes.setup: Trying to justify wearing bifocals
jokes.punchline: References an aging pop singer as style icon
jokes.explanation: Good specific cultural reference that shows Ross's desperation and poor understanding of cool.

jokes.text: Yeah, like a chimney. Big smoker. Big, big smoker.
jokes.setup: Lying about Ross being a smoker
jokes.punchline: Rachel's nervous over-selling of the lie
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Rachel's tendency to over-explain when lying.

jokes.text: I was just warming up the earpieces for you.
jokes.setup: Caught wearing someone else's glasses
jokes.punchline: Ross's ridiculous excuse
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe moment with absurd logic - completely implausible but delivered with Ross's characteristic earnestness.

jokes.text: Yes, it is. I was just moistening the tip.
jokes.setup: Caught with Dr. Greene's cigarette
jokes.punchline: Ross's innocent explanation has sexual undertones
jokes.explanation: Good accidental innuendo that fits Ross's pattern of innocent comments that sound inappropriate.

jokes.text: Everything you've heard is true.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if it's true the other party has beer
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dramatic confirmation like it's legendary
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic delivery treating beer availability like mythical treasure. Classic Chandler overstatement.

jokes.text: Joe, if these people find out that my spelling game's over there... there'll be nobody left at our party.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to keep people at her party
jokes.punchline: Admits her own party activities would cause mass exodus
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness from Monica - she knows her activities aren't fun but can't help herself.

jokes.text: All right, I'm sorry. But these people needed me. They work hard all week. It's Saturday night. They deserve to have a little fun. Go!
jokes.setup: Joey defending taking people to the fun party
jokes.punchline: His passionate defense of party guests' right to fun
jokes.explanation: Great Joey moment showing his genuine care for people having fun, delivered with unexpected passion.

jokes.text: It's a little quieter, less obvious sort of fun. But if people would just give it a chance, it's...
jokes.setup: Monica defending her party style
jokes.punchline: Her defensive rationalization trailing off as she realizes it's not working
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's defensive nature and growing realization that she might be wrong.

jokes.text: You've chosen a boyfriend exactly like your father.
jokes.setup: Mother-daughter therapy discussion
jokes.punchline: Classic psychological observation delivered bluntly
jokes.explanation: Standard psychology trope but fits the situation and advances the plot about Rachel's family dynamics.

jokes.text: I felt like Gulliver.
jokes.setup: Complaining about Rachel's mom spending on bonsai trees
jokes.punchline: Literary reference to feeling giant among tiny things
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference that's both economical and creates a vivid image of the situation.

jokes.text: What the hell does she want with half a boat?
jokes.setup: Complaining about divorce settlement
jokes.punchline: The absurd practicality of splitting a boat
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about divorce logistics - the absurdity of splitting indivisible assets.

jokes.text: I got a lot of 'champs,' 'chiefs,' 'sports.' I even got a 'governor.'
jokes.setup: Ross mentions getting called 'tiger' during his parents' divorce
jokes.punchline: Chandler's list of patronizing nicknames with escalating absurdity
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect rhythm. 'Governor' is an unexpected and absurd peak to the list.

jokes.text: She gets the house, he's in a condo my sister will decorate with wicker.
jokes.setup: Rachel predicting her future family dynamics
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about sister's terrible decorating taste
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail (wicker) that makes the prediction feel authentic and adds character depth.

jokes.text: I relied on a carefully regimented program... of denial... and wetting the bed.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking how he got through parents' divorce
jokes.punchline: Formal-sounding program that ends with childish behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection with perfect structure. The formal language building to 'wetting the bed' is expertly crafted and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Because it reminded you of the way... our forefathers used to bitch at each other?
jokes.setup: Rachel mentioning Fourth of July memories
jokes.punchline: Chandler's completely wrong interpretation connecting to American history
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist misdirection that connects family dysfunction to American history in an unexpected way.

jokes.text: It never occurred to anybody to bring another one.
jokes.setup: Describing family huddled under one blanket
jokes.punchline: The family's collective inability to solve simple problems
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about family dysfunction and the absurdity of obvious solutions everyone misses.

jokes.text: What are you doing? I'm getting ready for the water skiing.
jokes.setup: Dr. Greene making exaggerated departure gestures
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous explanation for physical preparation
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy implication with absurd justification for over-the-top goodbye behavior.

jokes.text: Sorry, we're on a major flan high.
jokes.setup: Explaining erratic behavior when parents almost meet
jokes.punchline: Blames it on sugar rush from dessert
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the flan running gag while providing absurd explanation for their frantic behavior.

jokes.text: Or perhaps you'd like a créme de menthe. I have to be heading toward my chateau.
jokes.setup: Dr. Greene's affected departure
jokes.punchline: Pretentious drink offer and location reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dr. Greene's upper-class pretensions and formality.

jokes.text: Yep! Get ready for the gelatinous fun.
jokes.setup: Monica announcing flan time
jokes.punchline: Ross's unenthusiastic description of flan's texture
jokes.explanation: Continues the flan mockery with emphasis on its unappetizing texture.

jokes.text: Looks like that stuff you get when you get an infection.
jokes.setup: Looking at the flan
jokes.punchline: Joey's graphic medical comparison
jokes.explanation: Very Joey-like gross observation that's both disgusting and unfortunately accurate.

jokes.text: Heads up! Wow! Those things almost never come true.
jokes.setup: Rachel making a wish and blowing out candles
jokes.punchline: Chandler interprets the wish literally coming true (flan flying)
jokes.explanation: Excellent physical comedy payoff with perfect misdirection. Great structure and highly quotable ending moment.

tmdb_overview: Monica plans a surprise birthday for Rachel that splits into 'two factions' when Rachel's estranged parents arrive unexpectedly.
